steps.title: Identify the radius
steps.content: Segment $QS$ is a diameter of the circle, and its length is $76$ millimeters. Segment $CR$ goes from the center $C$ to point $R$ on the circle, so $CR$ is a radius.

steps.title: Use half the diameter
steps.content: A radius is half the length of a diameter. Therefore, the length of $CR$ is $$CR=\frac{76}{2}=38$$

steps.title: State the requested length
steps.content: The requested segment is $CR$, so its length is $38$ millimeters. This is the radius of the circle with diameter $76$ millimeters. The answer is C.
